channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Iran FM speaks to Pakistan military chief, Iraqi Kurdistan President
item.description: French President Macron stated that West Asia's stability is in everyone's interest. Israel struck a Hezbollah missile launcher after Trump announced a three-week extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. Israel says it struck a Hezbollah missile launcher soon after Trump said the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire had been extended by three weeks.

item.title: Germany’s Merz says climate policy essential for global security, prosperity
item.description: German opposition leader Friedrich Merz stated that climate policy is crucial for global security and prosperity. He emphasized the need for effective climate action. Merz's comments highlight the intersection of environmental and geopolitical concerns.

item.title: Trump declines to give a timeline on ending war with Iran: ‘Don’t rush me’
item.description: US President Donald Trump declined to provide a timeline for ending the conflict with Iran. He urged patience, saying "don't rush me." Trump's remarks came amid escalating tensions between the US and Iran.

item.title: Pentagon Email Floats Suspending Spain From NATO, Other Steps Over Iran Rift: Report
item.description: A Pentagon email suggested suspending Spain from NATO due to its stance on Iran. The proposal was part of a range of measures being considered. The email's contents were reported by a US news outlet.

item.title: Lebanon truce extended; Trump says time not on Tehran’s side
item.description: A truce in Lebanon was extended, while US President Donald Trump said time is not on Iran's side. The comments were made amid heightened tensions between the US and Iran. The situation in Lebanon remains fragile.

item.title: EU Approves €90 Billion Ukraine Loan and Expands Sanctions on Russia
item.description: The European Union approved a €90 billion loan to Ukraine and expanded sanctions on Russia. The move aims to support Ukraine's economic stability and respond to Russia's actions. The EU's decision was reported by a diplomatic source.

item.title: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks but claims he won’t rush Iran deal
item.description: US President Trump announced a three-week extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. He also stated that he won't rush into an Iran deal, amid ongoing Middle East tensions. Middle East crisis live: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks but claims he won’t rush Iran deal The Guardian.

item.title: Trump invoking religion with Iran war may make things worse
item.description: Critics argue that Trump's invocation of religion in the Iran conflict may escalate tensions. This approach has sparked concerns about the potential consequences of the US's actions. Trump invoking religion with Iran war may make things worse | Opinion USA Today.

item.title: Briton in Netflix's 'Con Mum' faces fresh charges in Singapore
item.description: Dionne Marie Hanna, an 85-year-old Briton, faces new charges in Singapore for allegedly duping over two dozen people, including her own son. She was previously featured in a Netflix documentary.

item.title: India denounces ‘hellhole’ remark shared by Trump
item.description: India's Foreign Ministry condemned remarks by US radio host Michael Savage, shared by Trump, describing India as a "hellhole". The ministry called the comments "uninformed". India’s Foreign Ministry says comments by US radio host Michael Savage, circulated by Trump, are 'uninformed'.

item.title: Global hunger report warns of rising malnutrition and famine risks
item.description: A global hunger report warns of rising malnutrition and famine risks, with nearly 150 million people facing acute food shortages due to conflict. The crisis remains a pressing concern worldwide.

item.title: Kenyan leader sparks uproar after mocking Nigerians' spoken English
item.description: Kenyan President William Ruto sparked uproar after saying Kenyans speak "some of the best English in the world," while Nigerians' English is hard to understand. His comments drew criticism from Nigerians and Kenyans alike, with many calling for an apology.

item.title: War in the Middle East: latest developments
item.description: The Israeli military launched airstrikes in Gaza, while Hamas fired rockets into Israel. The conflict has resulted in multiple casualties on both sides, with no immediate end to the violence in sight.

item.title: UAE to deploy agentic AI across 50% of government sectors within two years
item.description: The UAE plans to deploy artificial intelligence across 50% of its government sectors within two years. The move aims to enhance efficiency and services, with AI expected to play a key role in decision-making processes.

item.title: Cars And Humanoid Robots: Elon Musk Lays Out Terafab AI Chip Project Plan
item.description: Elon Musk unveiled plans for the Terafab AI chip, which will handle each step of chip production, including design. The project aims to revolutionize the manufacturing process, with Musk touting its potential for increased efficiency and reduced costs.

item.title: Argument turns deadly at Mall of Louisiana: High school seniors among victims as FBI Director Kash
item.description: A shooting at the Mall of Louisiana left multiple people injured, including high school seniors. FBI Director Christopher Wray confirmed the agency is investigating the incident, with authorities working to determine the motive behind the attack.

item.title: 'Two ways of calculating': Trump Defends His Calculations On Drug Prices
item.description: US President Trump defended his calculations on drug prices at an event, using incorrect math. He made several such instances during the event, sparking scrutiny. It was one of several times Trump used his own - but incorrect - math during the drug pricing event.

item.title: Why is there a political deadlock in Lebanon?
item.description: A political deadlock exists in Lebanon due to the government's demand for Hezbollah to disarm. The goal is to establish a state monopoly on weapons. The Lebanese government wants Hezbollah to disarm as it aims for a state monopoly on weapons.

item.title: Trump says U.S. in no rush but 'clock is ticking' for Iran
item.description: US President Trump stated that the US is not in a rush regarding Iran, but the "clock is ticking". He had previously ruled out using a nuclear weapon against Iran.

item.title: China's Long-Awaited DeepSeek V4 AI Model Is Now Available For Preview
item.description: China's DeepSeek V4 AI model is now available for preview. It features an ultra-long context of one million words for advanced tasks. DeepSeek-V4 features an ultra-long context of one million words for advanced tasks.
